summary:
Occlusal trauma generally leads to permanent damage such as bone loss and gum recession; thus, it is not considered fully reversible clinically. The exception among given options highlights this principle accurately. To focus solely on core medical facts without extraneous details:

Occlusal trauma usually causes irreparable harm leading to long-term issues including bone resorption and gingival retreatment making full recovery unlikely; hence option B correctly identifies what does NOT apply here due its implication suggesting reversibility which contradicts established knowledge regarding persistent effects post-trauma events impacting oral health structures significantly
